observations.observation_value: In a rat high-severity chronic SCI model at Th10-Th11, Netrin-1 delivery to the lesion epicenter promoted recovery of hindlimb joint movement plus partial climbing/grasping and was associated with CST regrowth/sprouting/remyelination, extrapyramidal and serotonergic/dopaminergic axon reconnection, lower motoneuron preservation, and sensory-pathway neuroprotection.
observations.normalized_value: Netrin-1 promotes functional and anatomical repair signals in high-severity chronic rat SCI
observations.statistics_reported: PubMed abstract reports functional and anatomical endpoints; open full text not identified in metadata batch
observations.notes: Source-verified but not full-text upgraded; PMID 41163574.

observations.observation_value: CSPGs were identified as regulators of chronic scar maturation through a pro-inflammatory microglia-astrocyte axis; targeted CSPG degradation reprogrammed microglia toward an anti-inflammatory state and attenuated scar-forming astrocyte differentiation and fibrotic matrix deposition.

observations.statistics_reported: Full text reports CSPG-driven microglial inflammatory signaling, astrocyte scar transition, ChABC-based intervention, and matrix/scar endpoints
observations.notes: Full-text upgrade from PMID 41221600; cross-tags Module 3 immune-glial scar boundary.

observations.observation_value: In 106 participants with acute cervical SCI from the phase 2b NISCI trial, anti-Nogo-A NG101 treatment was associated with faster lesion-volume reduction and slower decline of cord area and tract-specific MTsat in corticospinal tracts and dorsal columns relative to placebo.

observations.statistics_reported: Full text reports trial MRI biomarkers including lesion volume, tissue bridges, CSA, and tract-specific MTsat over six months
observations.notes: Human trial imaging biomarker row; interpret as structural biomarker evidence, not direct axon-regrowth proof.

observations.observation_value: CITED2 was identified as an epigenetic switch active in immature/regenerating DRG neurons but silent after non-regenerative SCI; Cited2 overexpression reactivated growth programs and enhanced in vivo regeneration after SCI, while Cited2-targeting panobinostat promoted sensory and motor neuron growth/sprouting and functional recovery.
observations.normalized_value: CITED2/panobinostat reactivates neuronal growth programs after SCI
observations.statistics_reported: Full text reports developmental/regenerative DRG profiling, Cited2 gain/loss, in vivo SCI regeneration, pharmacologic HDAC inhibitor targeting, growth/sprouting, and functional endpoints
observations.notes: Full-text upgrade from PMID 41731079; intrinsic-growth epigenetic switch.

author_claims.claim_text: Corticospinal and descending-tract repair evidence includes regeneration, sprouting, plasticity, and functional recovery signals that should be separated.
author_claims.notes: First-pass claim abstraction; full text needed for effect sizes.

author_claims.claim_text: Optogenetic, magnetic, gabapentinoid, and molecular interventions can enhance CST-related growth or plasticity in selected models.
author_claims.notes: First-pass claim abstraction; full text needed for effect sizes.

author_claims.claim_text: CSPG, PTP-sigma, Nogo, Rho/ROCK, netrin, integrin, and laminin pathways define extrinsic growth-permissiveness boundaries.
author_claims.notes: First-pass claim abstraction; full text needed for effect sizes.

author_claims.claim_text: Matrix/guidance interventions often overlap with immune-glial scar and Schwann/cell-therapy mechanisms.
author_claims.notes: First-pass claim abstraction; full text needed for effect sizes.

author_claims.claim_text: Remyelination, myelin debris clearance, ventral-root pathology, and white matter repair form a distinct evidence family.
author_claims.notes: First-pass claim abstraction; full text needed for effect sizes.

author_claims.claim_text: Myelin debris clearance can support repair but may also be linked to chronic inflammation or fibrosis.
author_claims.notes: First-pass claim abstraction; full text needed for effect sizes.

author_claims.claim_text: Serotonergic, propriospinal, KCC2, and relay-pathway studies show repair can proceed via circuit reconstruction without literal CST regrowth.
author_claims.notes: First-pass claim abstraction; full text needed for effect sizes.

author_claims.claim_text: Relay and excitability restoration endpoints should be curated separately from anatomical regeneration endpoints.
author_claims.notes: First-pass claim abstraction; full text needed for effect sizes.

author_claims.claim_text: Intrinsic growth competence includes cellular energetics, transcriptional state, ciliogenesis, PTEN/mTOR, and maturation-linked epigenetic programs.
author_claims.notes: First-pass claim abstraction; full text needed for effect sizes.

author_claims.claim_text: Intrinsic-growth claims need neuron-type specificity and distinction between sprouting, regeneration, and survival.
author_claims.notes: First-pass claim abstraction; full text needed for effect sizes.

author_claims.claim_text: Grafts, scaffolds, laminin, Schwann/cell products, and biomaterials can enable axon repair but confound cell/material/molecular attribution.
author_claims.notes: First-pass claim abstraction; full text needed for effect sizes.

author_claims.claim_text: Graft-enabled CST regeneration should be cross-tagged with Module 4 transplantation and Module 2 biomaterials.
author_claims.notes: First-pass claim abstraction; full text needed for effect sizes.

author_claims.claim_text: Activity, stimulation, and training can amplify molecular repair programs and should be treated as combination interventions.
author_claims.notes: First-pass claim abstraction; full text needed for effect sizes.

author_claims.claim_text: Treadmill/OPN/IGF-1R/Akt/mTOR evidence bridges intrinsic growth and activity-dependent plasticity.
author_claims.notes: First-pass claim abstraction; full text needed for effect sizes.

author_claims.claim_text: Module 5 consensus must distinguish CST, RST, sensory, propriospinal, serotonergic, and white matter endpoints.
author_claims.notes: First-pass claim abstraction; full text needed for effect sizes.

author_claims.claim_text: Functional recovery does not by itself identify whether repair occurred through regeneration, sprouting, remyelination, relay, or compensation.
author_claims.notes: First-pass claim abstraction; full text needed for effect sizes.

author_claims.claim_text: The saturated 5A corpus supports mechanism-family consensus profiles rather than one pooled axon-regeneration efficacy claim.
author_claims.notes: First-pass claim abstraction; full text needed for effect sizes.

author_claims.claim_text: Human ventral-root and motoneuron pathology should constrain translation from rodent remyelination and axon-repair models.
author_claims.notes: First-pass claim abstraction; full text needed for effect sizes.

author_claims.claim_text: Netrin/DCC and NgR/RhoA/ROCK evidence should be curated as guidance plus inhibitory-pathway modulation.
author_claims.notes: First-pass claim abstraction; full text needed for effect sizes.

author_claims.claim_text: Propriospinal detours and KCC2-enabled relay pathways are circuit-level repair mechanisms.
author_claims.notes: First-pass claim abstraction; full text needed for effect sizes.

author_claims.claim_text: TTBK2-driven ciliogenesis is a candidate intrinsic neuronal regeneration program.
author_claims.notes: First-pass claim abstraction; full text needed for effect sizes.

author_claims.claim_text: CITED2 links neuronal maturation to regenerative decline and is a candidate epigenetic repair switch.
author_claims.notes: First-pass claim abstraction; full text needed for effect sizes.

author_claims.claim_text: Lesion-remote astrocyte-microglia control of white matter repair cross-links Module 5 with Module 3.
author_claims.notes: First-pass claim abstraction; full text needed for effect sizes.

author_claims.claim_text: Oligodendrogenic progenitors plus chondroitinase ABC cross-link remyelination, inhibitory matrix digestion, and cell transplantation.
author_claims.notes: First-pass claim abstraction; full text needed for effect sizes.

author_claims.claim_text: Thiorphan-like neuronal reprogramming suggests pharmacologic growth-state manipulation can improve functional recovery.
author_claims.notes: First-pass claim abstraction; full text needed for effect sizes.

author_claims.claim_text: Large-animal laminin therapy supports translational relevance but should remain feasibility/context evidence until full endpoints are extracted.
author_claims.notes: First-pass claim abstraction; full text needed for effect sizes.

author_claims.claim_text: Female-rat TMS evidence should retain sex/model details in the outcome vocabulary.
author_claims.notes: First-pass claim abstraction; full text needed for effect sizes.

author_claims.claim_text: Single-cell regeneration signatures are mapping evidence unless tied to perturbation and tract-specific outcomes.
author_claims.notes: First-pass claim abstraction; full text needed for effect sizes.

author_claims.claim_text: Remyelination-deficient models help separate compensation from true myelin repair.
author_claims.notes: First-pass claim abstraction; full text needed for effect sizes.

author_claims.claim_text: Cellular energetics is a core intrinsic-growth axis and should not be merged with trophic support alone.
author_claims.notes: First-pass claim abstraction; full text needed for effect sizes.

author_claims.claim_text: Scaffold-supported motor recovery requires material, tract, and behavioral endpoint separation.
author_claims.notes: First-pass claim abstraction; full text needed for effect sizes.

author_claims.claim_text: Full-text upgrades should prioritize anchors that directly test tract anatomy alongside functional behavior.
author_claims.notes: First-pass claim abstraction; full text needed for effect sizes.

author_claims.claim_text: Synaptotagmin 4 supports spontaneous sprouting and should be distinguished from experimentally induced regeneration.
author_claims.notes: First-pass claim abstraction; full text needed for effect sizes.

author_claims.claim_text: Serotonergic-targeted hydrogel evidence cross-links circuit excitability and biomaterial delivery.
author_claims.notes: First-pass claim abstraction; full text needed for effect sizes.

author_claims.claim_text: Species and chronicity boundaries are essential before comparing chronic scar, acute injury, dog, human pathology, and rodent repair models.
author_claims.notes: First-pass claim abstraction; full text needed for effect sizes.

author_claims.claim_text: Consensus wording should avoid treating all axon growth, remyelination, and circuit compensation as the same repair process.
author_claims.notes: First-pass claim abstraction; full text needed for effect sizes.

boundary_records.interpretation_rule: Sprouting, regeneration, plasticity, and functional recovery require separate labels.
Matrix/guidance interventions and neuron-intrinsic growth programs test different repair axes.
Remyelination, debris clearance, oligodendrocyte support, and white matter repair should not be merged with axon regeneration.
Serotonergic, propriospinal, KCC2, and relay mechanisms can restore function without direct long-tract regrowth.
Grafts, scaffolds, chondroitinase, cells, stimulation, and training confound single-component attribution.
Human pathology and dog therapy anchors constrain rodent repair extrapolation.
Module 5 overlaps with lesion architecture, immune state, cell transplantation, and biomaterials.
Rows preserve first-pass evidence direction and topic placement, not quantitative effect sizes.

consensus_drafts.draft_statement: Descending-tract repair evidence must distinguish regeneration, sprouting, plasticity, and behavioral recovery.
consensus_drafts.consensus_statement: Descending-tract repair evidence must distinguish regeneration, sprouting, plasticity, and behavioral recovery.

consensus_drafts.draft_statement: Extrinsic matrix and guidance-pathway interventions define environmental permissiveness, but overlap with scar, immune, and cell-therapy mechanisms.
consensus_drafts.consensus_statement: Extrinsic matrix and guidance-pathway interventions define environmental permissiveness, but overlap with scar, immune, and cell-therapy mechanisms.

consensus_drafts.draft_statement: Remyelination, myelin debris clearance, and white matter repair are distinct from axon regeneration and require separate consensus profiles.
consensus_drafts.consensus_statement: Remyelination, myelin debris clearance, and white matter repair are distinct from axon regeneration and require separate consensus profiles.

consensus_drafts.draft_statement: Relay, serotonergic, propriospinal, and KCC2 mechanisms support circuit reconstruction as an alternative to direct long-tract regrowth.
consensus_drafts.consensus_statement: Relay, serotonergic, propriospinal, and KCC2 mechanisms support circuit reconstruction as an alternative to direct long-tract regrowth.

consensus_drafts.draft_statement: Intrinsic growth competence includes metabolic, transcriptional, epigenetic, PTEN/mTOR, and ciliogenesis programs that should be separated from environmental repair.
consensus_drafts.consensus_statement: Intrinsic growth competence includes metabolic, transcriptional, epigenetic, PTEN/mTOR, and ciliogenesis programs that should be separated from environmental repair.

consensus_drafts.draft_statement: Graft and scaffold-enabled axon repair is promising but must be cross-tagged with cell-transplant and biomaterial mechanisms.
consensus_drafts.consensus_statement: Graft and scaffold-enabled axon repair is promising but must be cross-tagged with cell-transplant and biomaterial mechanisms.

consensus_drafts.draft_statement: Activity, stimulation, and training can amplify repair programs and should be modeled as combination interventions.
consensus_drafts.consensus_statement: Activity, stimulation, and training can amplify repair programs and should be modeled as combination interventions.

consensus_drafts.draft_statement: Module 5 supports mechanism-specific repair profiles rather than one pooled axon-regeneration efficacy claim.
consensus_drafts.consensus_statement: Module 5 supports mechanism-specific repair profiles rather than one pooled axon-regeneration efficacy claim.
